title: poa caesia var. glauca
intro: Poa caesia var. glauca is a variety of the Poa caesia plant, commonly known as blue fescue, which is a type of grass native to New Zealand. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, as well as reduce fever and alleviate digestive issues. The health benefits of Poa caesia var. glauca include its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve overall cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Poa caesia var. glauca can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are typically consumed as a hot or cold drink to reap its medicinal benefits.
